generated_pair.question: What is the primary purpose of the Python Standard Library as described in the document?
generated_pair.answer: The Python Standard Library describes the standard library that is distributed with Python and some optional components commonly included in Python distributions. It provides built-in modules and standardized solutions for many problems in everyday programming.
supporting_sentences: this library reference manual describes the standard library that is distributed with Python. It also describes some of the optional components that are commonly included in Python distributions.

The library contains built-in modules (written in C) that provide access to system functionality such as file I/O that would otherwise be inaccessible to Python programmers, as well as modules written in Python that provide standardized solutions for many problems that occur in everyday programming.

generated_pair.question: How is Python typically provided on Unix-like operating systems?
generated_pair.answer: On Unix-like operating systems, Python is normally provided as a collection of packages, and it may be necessary to use the packaging tools provided with the operating system to obtain some or all of the optional components.
